mission.title: operator-framework: [ansible] CR variables converted to snake_case when passed to playbook
mission.description: [ansible] CR variables converted to snake_case when passed to playbook. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current operator-framework deployment
steps.description:
Verify your operator-framework version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working operator-framework installation.

steps.title: Review operator-framework configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant operator-framework configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get configmap -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=operator-framework
```
**What did you do?**

I have a CR yaml that contains camelCase key names. When the ansible playbook is called, all keys are converted to snake_case.

steps.title: Apply the fix for [ansible] CR variables converted to snake_case when passed…
steps.description:
for a resource named:
```yaml
- version: v1alpha1
  group: softwarefactory-project.io
  kind: Zuul
```
You can access the unmodified spec with this ansible var: `_softwarefactory-project_io_zuul_spec`, at least with quay.io/operator-framework/ansible-operator:v0.13.0
```yaml
$ kubectl version
Client Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"14", GitVersion:"v1.14.2", GitCommit:"66049e3b21efe110454d67df4fa62b08ea79a19b", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2019-05-16T16:23:09Z", GoVersion:"go1.12.5", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"}
Server Version: version.Info{Major:"1", Minor:"13+", GitVersion:"v1.13.4+6569b4f", GitCommit:"6569b4f", GitTreeState:"clean", BuildDate:"2019-07-10T19:31:33Z", GoVersion:"go1.11.6", Compiler:"gc", Platform:"linux/amd64"}
```

steps.title: Upgrade operator-framework to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade operator-framework:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade operator-framework operator-framework/operator-framework --namespace operator-framework
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework
helm list -n operator-framework
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n operator-framework -l app.kubernetes.io/name=operator-framework
kubectl get events -n operator-framework --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "[ansible] CR variables converted to snake_case when passed…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
for a resource named:
```yaml
- version: v1alpha1
  group: softwarefactory-project.io
  kind: Zuul
```
You can access the unmodified spec with this ansible var: `_softwarefactory-project_io_zuul_spec`, at least with quay.io/operator-framework/ansible-operator:v0.13.0
codeSnippets:
deployment:
    version: "1.0"
    affinity:
      pod:
        preferredDuringSchedulingIgnoredDuringExecution:
        - weight: 100
          podAffinityTerm:
            labelSelector:
              matchExpressions:
              - key: security
                operator: In
                values:
                - S2
            topologyKey: failure-domain.beta.kubernetes.io/zone

- debug:
    msg: "STARTING OPTEST: deployment is: {{ deployment }}"

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with operator-framework installed or the issue environment reproducible.
